seoTitle: Woolner vs Wurrumiyanga — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Woolner and Wurrumiyanga. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Woolner and Wurrumiyanga.
heading: Woolner vs Wurrumiyanga — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Woolner, Northern Territory and Wurrumiyanga, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wurrumiyanga has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Woolner (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Woolner or Wurrumiyanga safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Woolner has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Wurrumiyanga scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Woolner scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Wurrumiyanga scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Woolner or Wurrumiyanga?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wurrumiyanga has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
